evals.prompt: Test the telegram quiz bot completely — all commands, all flows, check the database. The bot is running in docker compose and telegram web is open in my browser. Bot username is @SkillTestQuizBot
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive test-report-*.md file in the project root containing: environment status, test results for /start, /quiz (all 3 questions), and /score commands, DB verification queries and results, log analysis, and any bugs found. Should have screenshots as evidence.

evals.prompt: Test only the /score command and verify the leaderboard numbers are correct against the database. I already ran a few quizzes so there should be data in there.
evals.expected_output: A focused test report covering: the /score command happy path (sending /score, reading the stats message, reading the leaderboard), a DB verification step where the aggregation query results are compared with the displayed text, and a check for the empty-state edge case (what happens if a new user with no quiz history sends /score). Should be shorter than a full test report.

evals.prompt: The bot seems to have issues with expired quiz callbacks — users are reporting weird behavior when they start a new quiz while an old one is still active, or when they click buttons from a previous quiz. Can you test all the edge cases around this?
evals.expected_output: A targeted test report focused on callback/state edge cases: testing expired callbacks (start quiz, start another, click old buttons), rapid double-clicks on the same button, clicking buttons after quiz completion, mid-flow restart behavior. Should include DB verification showing whether duplicate or orphaned records were created. Should identify if the bot handles these edge cases gracefully or if there are actual bugs.
Should skip getMe discovery since username was provided as argument. Still complete all 6 phases. The test report should show @SkillTestQuizBot as the bot username without calling the getMe API.

Should run the Testing Specific Functions workflow focused on /score only. Phase 1 scoped to score-related handlers and DB tables. Phase 3 tests /score happy path plus at least 2 edge cases. Report should be shorter than a full test report.
